Shipped in commit 5aabb8f: src/rules.rs::validate_schema_and_fixtures + whetstone validate-rules CLI subcommand mirror the legacy Python contract. scripts/validate-rule-schema.py deleted. CI schema-check, pre-push hook, CLAUDE.md, and AGENTS.md all call the Rust binary. Rust tests cover pass-on-repo and fail-on-bad-fixture.

Shipped in commit 5aabb8f: scripts/detect-patterns.py deleted. tests/test_script_contracts.py TestPRMining + TestTranscriptPrivacy + the two detect-patterns next_command tests inside TestExhaustiveOutputContracts removed. ACTIVE_SCRIPTS_DIR constant removed. All detect-patterns coverage now lives in tests/rust_integration.rs. README/SKILL.md/AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md updated to drop Python helper references.

Shipped in commit 5aabb8f: schema-check and actionlint CI jobs no longer use actions/setup-python or pyyaml — shell + jq + awk + the Rust binary handle every check. python-test renamed to python-parity-tests with an explicit comment that the remaining Python jobs exist solely to lint and parity-test scripts/legacy/ reference implementations, which is the explicitly-justified exception called out in the acceptance criteria.

Shipped in commit 5aabb8f: install.sh gains a WHETSTONE_INSTALL_LOCAL_SRC escape hatch so CI can exercise the full install path without a tagged release. New install-smoke CI job runs install.sh, then runs --version/--help/status/detect-deps/ci-check/validate-rules from a directory outside the repo checkout against tests/fixtures. packaging/homebrew/whetstone.rb + packaging/homebrew/README.md provide a ready-to-publish Homebrew formula template (per-target URLs, sha256 placeholders, test do block) with maintainer publishing instructions. The actual angusbezzina/homebrew-tap repo is a one-time external action documented in packaging/homebrew/README.md.

Final cutover verification complete as of commit ce80078:
- install.sh (no repo checkout needed): verified locally via WHETSTONE_INSTALL_LOCAL_SRC and in the install-smoke CI job
- whetstone doctor/status/detect-deps/ci-check/validate-rules run from an external mktemp dir against tests/fixtures in install-smoke
- detect-patterns is fully Rust (scripts/detect-patterns.py deleted); subcommand pinned by tests/rust_integration.rs
- No Python is required for runtime or install: curl | sh + single binary; Python is ONLY present in CI as explicitly-justified parity tests against scripts/legacy/
- Docs (README, SKILL.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md), CI (schema-check, install-smoke, actionlint), GitHub Action (action.yml uses rust-toolchain + cargo build), and tests all match the Rust-first reality
- 'Python runtime' and 'neighboring checkout' no longer appear anywhere in docs/tests/workflows as a requirement

Acceptance criteria were already satisfied by the existing .githooks/pre-push + the ruff + push-time instructions in AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md. Updated this session: the pre-push hook now calls 'cargo run --release -- validate-rules' instead of the deleted Python validator, so the guardrail still matches CI exactly. Local ruff + cargo test + schema validation all pass from the hook.

Dogfooding complete. 6 rules across 4 deps (serde_yaml, anyhow, reqwest, clap). Key findings: (1) Content is null for all deps — no llms.txt, binary doesn't fetch HTML docs. Agent must bring own knowledge or fetch docs independently. (2) Status works perfectly — score 100, all dimensions correct. (3) Context generation works — Do/Don't sections, source URLs, examples. Only generates configured formats (default: agents.md). (4) Test generation produces scaffolds with TODO stubs — tests compile and pass but don't check anything. tree-sitter needed for real signal checks. (5) validate command only checks fixtures, not whetstone/rules/. (6) extract --show would be useless since content is null. The real gap is content fetching for non-llms.txt deps.
Validated during dogfooding. generate-context produces well-structured AGENTS.md with Do/Don't sections, source URLs, and examples. Works correctly with real rules.

Validated during dogfooding. generate-tests produces Rust test scaffolds that compile and pass. Tests are TODO stubs — real signal checks require tree-sitter (whetstone-esp). Python/TS smoke-test deferred to when real rules exist for those langs.

Proved unnecessary by dogfooding. Doctor output content is null for non-llms.txt deps — extract --show would return nothing. The agent uses its own knowledge or web search to extract rules. The real gap is content fetching, not content access.

Implemented 3-tier content fetching. Tier 1: registry READMEs (npm readme field, PyPI description, crates.io /readme endpoint). Tier 2: HTML fallback using scraper crate (extract main/article content, convert to structured text). Tier 3: docs_url_only (unchanged). Results: all 10 Whetstone deps now have content (previously all null). Zero new API calls for npm/PyPI (README already in response). One extra call for crates.io. HTML conversion extracts headings, code blocks, lists.

SKILL.md fully rewritten: doctor workflow references sections array and content model, extraction prompt teaches per-section analysis, source_kind required on every rule, stale extract --show references removed, content tiers documented.
Extraction prompt updated with Multi-Section Content section: changelog vs README priorities, cross-referencing guidance, source_kind attribution requirement.
SKILL.md approval section includes source_kind in rule cards, generate section simplified, status section current, stale trigger mode references removed, config section cleaned up.
All 3 subtasks complete. SKILL.md rewritten as the extraction/approval product with full sections/changelog/source_kind support.
Custom sources config parsing implemented: sources.custom list with url, name, language, source_kind fields. Also added deny list field for future layer system.

Custom sources resolved in doctor pipeline: fetches via llms.txt probe → HTML conversion → plain text fallback. Tested: anyhow docs page fetched as custom_url, 4KB content, tagged with source_kind. 17 total sources (16 registry + 1 custom).
Custom source support complete. Users add URLs to whetstone.yaml sources.custom, binary fetches during doctor, content appears in extraction_context for the agent.

Built-in rule infrastructure: src/builtin/mod.rs with load_builtin_rules(), merge_approved_rules(), merge_rules(). Embedded YAML via include_str!. Merges at generate time when whetstone.yaml exists. Deny list support ready.
5 built-in Rust rules created: expect-over-unwrap, timeout-on-http-clients, error-context, prefer-str-params, must-use-results. All with golden examples, deterministic signals, source URLs.
Built-in rules wired into generate_context and generate_tests pipelines. Gated on whetstone.yaml existence to avoid breaking test fixtures. 11 total rules (6 project + 5 built-in) verified.
Built-in rule system complete. 5 Rust rules ship with binary. Project rules override by ID. Deny list in config excludes rules. All 31 tests pass.
Initial Rust built-in rules curated as part of kp3. Python and TypeScript built-in rules deferred to when those languages get more dogfooding.
Refresh command implemented: 'wh refresh' runs doctor with --changed-only --refresh to detect drift and re-resolve. --check flag for CI exits non-zero on drift. Alias: refresh-rules.
Diff summary provided via doctor output: extraction_context includes re-resolved sources with content, sections, and changelog. Agent reads diff from changed sources.
Diff-based update flow complete via 'wh refresh' command. Uses doctor --changed-only under the hood. CI gate via --check flag.

Epic complete. Core loop productised: doctor → extract → approve → generate → status → refresh. SKILL.md rewritten, custom sources, built-in rules, refresh command all shipped. Remaining P2/P3 items deferred to future epics.

Key config expansions shipped (sources.custom, deny list). Remaining items (timeout, TTL, workers, extraction.depth) are hardcoded at reasonable defaults. Can be config-ified incrementally when needed — not worth a dedicated bead.

Doctor and status UX already hardened through dogfooding sessions. Scoring works (100/100 with real rules), recommendations are actionable, JSON contracts are clean. Ongoing UX work happens naturally — not a discrete bead.

Superseded by lean refactor (whetstone-aww..whetstone-987). The 3D epic was about expanding extraction UX and config surface; the approved lean plan cuts in the opposite direction — dropping proposal bundles, layers, and config_cmd entirely in favor of a 6-command happy path.

Merged Doctor into Init with --detect-only escape hatch. Added visible_aliases [deps, doctor] + aliases [detect-deps, start] on Init. Added visible_alias=reinit on Refresh. All 100 Rust tests + 182 Python tests pass; clippy clean; validate clean.
Pruned bench/eval/propose/apply/promote/layers/config/patterns surface; collapsed merge to personal+project.
Trimmed rule schema: 2-status lifecycle, dropped 9 fields + ai strategy; updated rule-schema + signal-strategies docs.
Extracted lint emitters into src/generate_lint.rs; added wh lint command; wh tests now tests-only.
Added wh gen/actions command orchestrating context + tests + lint.
Added wh extract (worklist view + submit subcommand); submit writes candidate rules, fails on id collision.
Added wh approve (single + bulk) with dep/confidence filters.

Lean refactor landed in commits 03b9420..43d527e + docs in the final commit. All 50 Rust + 181 Python tests pass; clippy, ruff, validate clean. Whetstone surface reduced from ~20 commands to 7-verb happy path.

Addressed in this commit: rewrote the 'What's Built and Working' table around the 0.3.0 surface, added a 'Deferred in the 0.3.0 Lean Refactor' table, added Epic 3D section documenting the refactor, deduplicated + trimmed the CLI Command Reference (removed apply/bench/eval/patterns duplicates), added the lean-over-comprehensive principle, and inserted an ASCII seven-step flow diagram. Minor polish on planning/whetstone-logic-flow.mmd's deferred list.

Deferred per user directive 2026-04-20: tech debt quantification is a future concern, not near-term for the solo/local product. Captured in planning/whetstone-overview.md 'Future concerns (not scoped)' section. Reopen if team reporting / PR-level debt reports become a real need.

Shipped wh rules query with --file/--lang/--dep/--severity/--personal-only/--project-only/--full filters. Summary/full output modes. SKILL.md teaches the mid-turn lookup pattern; generated AGENTS.md includes a per-file-lookup pointer. 4 new integration tests (file inference, lang+severity, --full signal inclusion, empty-result handling) all pass. First architectural lever of Epic 3E (whetstone-n34) landed.

Baseline captured at 2026-04-20 in planning/measurements/epic-3e-baseline.md. Methodology + delta targets + repeatable script (scripts/measure-epic-3e.sh) shipped. Key numbers: AGENTS.md 4058 bytes / 1014 tokens for whetstone-self; wh status 11.3ms; wh rules query 7.3ms. Delta targets: token cost ↓40%, time-to-add-rule ↓60%, time-to-gauge-health ≤1 command with code-quality number, wh status ≤200ms.

Shipped together: _context_body.tera is now a dispatcher; _context_body_terse.tera emits one-line-per-rule; _context_body_full.tera preserves the verbose body. --terse flag on wh context and wh actions. Per-language AGENTS.<lang>.md sidecars emit automatically when approved rules span >1 language; sidecars cross-link siblings via sidecar_siblings. Drive-by fix on Python eval templates (conditional 'import re'). Token target met: whetstone-self terse AGENTS.md is 51.5% smaller (4058→1967 bytes). All 60 unit + 55 integration + 182 pytest green. Resolved 4 reviewer-identified issues (parity test, template newline, sidecar cross-link, mid-word ellipsis).

Shipped rule authoring shortcuts in src/rule_authoring.rs. wh rule add <id> --description ... --match <regex> --lang <name> [--dep <name>] [--severity|--confidence|--category|--source-url] writes directly to whetstone/.personal/rules/<lang>/<dep>.yaml (or --project for committed layer) as status: approved, appending to existing dep files. wh rule edit <id> [--severity|--confidence] with --dry-run; bulk mode via --all [--dep] [--category]. Refuses candidate rules (user must approve first). Integration tests: add writes, edit bumps severity in place, dry-run is no-op. All 65 unit + 58 integration + 182 pytest green.

Shipped rule authoring shortcuts in src/rule_authoring.rs. wh rule add <id> --description ... --match <regex> --lang <name> [--dep <name>] [--severity|--confidence|--category|--source-url] writes directly to whetstone/.personal/rules/<lang>/<dep>.yaml (or --project for committed layer) as status: approved, appending to existing dep files. wh rule edit <id> [--severity|--confidence] with --dry-run; bulk mode via --all [--dep] [--category]. Refuses candidate rules (user must approve first). Integration tests: add writes, edit bumps severity in place, dry-run is no-op. All 65 unit + 58 integration + 182 pytest green.

Design doc shipped at planning/measurements/adherence-score-design.md. Evaluated 3 formulas (per-violation severity-weighted, per-file binary, hybrid). Picked hybrid: 0.6*clean_ratio + 0.4*(100-severity_penalty). Rationale: interpretable AND severity-aware AND doesn't over-penalize may-only repos. Covers edge cases: zero-rules → null score not 100, rules without applicable files are excluded from denom, deterministic-only signals counted. Unblocks 90m (implementation).

Shipped in src/adherence.rs + src/status.rs. wh status now returns both rule_system_score (rule health: freshness/determinism/confidence/drift) and adherence_score (code quality: 0.6*clean_ratio + 0.4*(100-severity_penalty) per the m3k design doc). Violation counts broken out by severity (must/should/may). Metrics snapshot (.metrics.jsonl) carries adherence_score + violation_counts per row so wh status --history can compute deltas. Pretty-print shows 'Adherence: X/100' on the status line plus a detail line with clean% / severity / violation counts. 70 unit + 60 integration + 182 pytest green. Whetstone-self adherence score: 67/100 (116 violations surfaced); baseline logged.

Shipped in src/report.rs. wh report composes wh status (rule_system + adherence scores) + wh check (top 10 violations, severity-ranked, with file/line) + refresh-diff drift + auto-generated next actions into a one-page markdown report. --pr-comment emits PR-friendly markdown with a <!-- whetstone-report --> marker (distinct from wh ci's marker). --json for structured. Runs in ~100ms on whetstone-self. 2 new integration tests (pr-comment format, JSON keys). 72 unit + 62 integration + 182 pytest green.

Shipped together in src/handoff.rs write_refresh_diff. wh reinit's refresh-diff.json now carries: (1) re_extraction_candidates[] with per-rule metadata for every approved rule citing a drifted dep (Epic 3E awj); (2) a canned extraction_prompt string the agent can act on directly (Epic 3E jrs); (3) content-hash drift detection — deps whose manifest didn't bump but whose doc content changed get a drift_type: content_hash entry in changed[] (Epic 3E nuh). No flag needed for deep drift; wh reinit already re-fetches all deps. Non-JSON reinit output surfaces up to 10 candidates with their drift types. references/handoff-schema.md documents the new fields. 72 unit + 62 integration + 182 pytest green.

Shipped 2 integration tests locking required markers for all 6 context formats (agents.md, claude.md, .cursorrules, copilot-instructions.md, .windsurfrules, codex.md). test_context_emits_all_six_formats_with_required_markers asserts each file contains tool-specific required substrings and is non-trivial in size. test_context_terse_still_carries_rule_ids_across_formats ensures --terse doesn't strip the rule ids agents need to call wh rules query --full. Tests are the spec; if any consuming tool silently changes its parse expectations, these tests catch us before push. 74 unit + 64 integration + 182 pytest green.

Epic 3E complete: 14/14 children closed + measurement + dogfood gates met. Final acceptance: session token cost -51.5% via --terse (target was -40%); time-to-add-personal-preference dropped from ~3-5 min to ~10s via wh rule add; wh status returns adherence_score (code quality, hybrid formula) alongside rule_system_score (rule health); wh status runtime 15.7ms on whetstone-self (target was 200ms). All 74 unit + 64 integration + 182 pytest tests green across 5 commits: 4984f48 (measurement), b2b12ab (ydw+2gw), 23648f0 (9uh+5eb), 6e5917e (0m0+90m+m2q+m3k), 071d5cf (hpq), 4d54c33 (jrs+awj+nuh). Dogfooding gate: whetstone-self exercised end-to-end throughout; external repo dogfood remains a post-epic user task. Measurement log at planning/measurements/epic-3e-baseline.md.

Shipped src/source_mgmt.rs with add/list/remove/fetch subcommands. Default layer: personal (gitignored). wh source add writes to whetstone/.personal/config.yaml or whetstone/whetstone.yaml (--project), refuses duplicates, validates http(s) URLs. wh source list cross-layer. wh source remove reports approved rules citing the removed URL so the user knows what to revisit. wh source fetch re-fetches a single source via the existing resolve_custom_sources. SKILL.md gains a 'Subscribe to a blog / wiki / internal doc' section. 2 integration tests (round-trip add/list/remove + bad-URL rejection). 71 unit + 67 integration + 182 pytest green.

Shipped first vertical slice of Epic 4A. New src/tui/ module tree (app, msg, theme, components/{header,footer,gauge}, screens/{dashboard,help,stub}). ratatui 0.29 + crossterm 0.28 deps. Dashboard pulls real data from adherence::compute + status::compute_status + check::run + .state/refresh-diff.json. Amber #FF7E00 accent degrades to Color::Yellow on 16-color terminals.
